content_type: retro pixel-art animated Christmas card / meme spectacle
summary: An official ICE Christmas video rendered as a nostalgic 16-bit, pixel-art animation: a blue-and-white 'ICE AIR' jetliner (a wink at deportation flights) takes off and banks over a snow-covered Christmas village with glowing lights and falling snow, its tail decorated with a holiday wreath. The clip builds to a sparkling, light-bulb-style 'Merry Christmas from ICE' title card. With no spoken transcript and a high music score, it is a music-driven, stylized meme/greeting-card spectacle designed to be cute, ironic, and highly shareable.

script.scene: Pixel-art 'ICE AIR' airliner climbs steeply to the right over a snowy nighttime Christmas village, heavy snowflakes falling; a red-and-green holiday wreath decorates the tail fin.
The plane levels off lower across the frame as the glowing, light-strung village with churches and cottages scrolls beneath it amid falling snow.
Wide shot of the lit village under snowfall with evergreen trees in the foreground; the 'ICE AIR' jet descends toward the lower-left, skimming past the rooftops.
Title card: sparkling, marquee-bulb lettering reads 'Merry Christmas from ICE' over the snowy village, with the small 'ICE AIR' plane flying above the text and snowflakes twinkling.
The 'ICE AIR' jetliner banks gracefully to the right against the dark snowy sky above the glowing village, closing the loop of the animation.

notable_text: On-aircraft livery: 'ICE AIR'. Title card: 'Merry Christmas from ICE'. Tweet text: 'Merry Christmas, America!'
